topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on the entryway table, a stark splash of vibrant color against the muted tones of the hallway. Inside, the petals of deep crimson roses were impossibly perfect, wrapped in tissue paper that felt strangely cool beneath my fingertips. There was no note. Just the flowers. My breath hitched in my throat as I approached it, my hand hovering over the wrapping. Every instinct screamed at me to turn away, to slam the lid shut and forget the whole unsettling experience. I could feel the blood thrumming in my ears, a frantic rhythm against the sudden stillness of the apartment. Who would send these? And why leave me with this silent, opulent offering? A tremor ran through my hands, making the paper crinkle loudly.

[story 2]

I found the arrangement tucked beside my laptop on the kitchen counter. They were lilies, blindingly white and impossibly fragrant, their stems tied with a thin, silver ribbon. I stared at them, my gaze darting around the room as if expecting someone to materialize from behind the refrigerator. My palms grew slick, and I gripped the edge of the counter until my knuckles turned white. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. This wasn't a friendly gesture; it felt invasive, a silent declaration that I was being watched. I slowly reached out, my fingers brushing the velvety edges of a petal, and then yanked my hand back as if burned.

[story 3]

The package arrived during the dead of night, slipped under the door with a soft thud. When I finally retrieved it, the scent of gardenia was overpowering, thick and heavy in the confined space of my bedroom. The bouquet was extravagant—orchids in shades of violet and emerald, so lush they seemed almost unreal. I stood by the window, peering out at the dark street, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. I didn't dare move toward the flowers. My feet felt rooted to the floorboards; every muscle in my body was tense, coiled tight, anticipating some unseen consequence. The silence of the room amplified the frantic drumming within my chest.
